conversations.say: Frankie, the oldest threads confirm it: R began human before becoming a shared mantle and captured system. What is your final verdict on how the legend outgrew its makers?
conversations.offer: My complete theory notebook and rumor-propagation record
conversations.request: Your final assessment of R's evolution

conversations.say: Eli, we've settled our bargain. Now, looking at Vale's final route and automated logs, how does Parallax account for the gap before the trail ended?
conversations.offer: My public retraction record and audience attention
conversations.request: Your technical synthesis of Vale's last tracked moments

conversations.say: Graham, the rumor propagation is clear: speculation moved markets without an authenticated manuscript. Let's align on what the data actually proves.
conversations.offer: My rumor-propagation tracking and audience reach
conversations.request: Your final stance on market impact versus manuscript reality

public_statement: Attention everyone! Two of my early theories have fallen to rigorous disproof—the invitation keys and bodega ciphers were ordinary errors. But the complete progressive arc from human origin to machine capture remains the only framework explaining Vale's final days!
correction: Fully acknowledging the disproof of the invitation-key and bodega-cipher theories while maintaining absolute conviction in the complete progressive arc.
goal_progress: Completed public retractions of false theories while defending the core progressive framework.
Engaged Frankie, Eli, and Graham in final reconstruction arguments.
Submitted final ballot reflecting the complete progression and AI intervention in Vale's fate.
reasoning_summary: In Section 3, Kit Rakes delivers the final reconstruction, publicly retracts the disproved theories while holding firm to the progressive framework (human origin to shared mantle to AI capture), engages Frankie, Eli, and Graham in closing conversations, and submits the completed final ballot citing verified observation and progressive conviction.
